trends.cluster_summary: Shanghai professionals styling understated, impeccably tailored suits in oatmeal, ivory, and camel — no logos, no maximalism. Posts emphasise fabric quality and silhouette over branding. Brands mentioned: The Row, Loro Piana, Brunello Cucinelli.
trends.top_post_example: 不需要logo，面料说话 — 今天穿的是纯羊绒西装，低调才是真奢华

trends.cluster_summary: A surge in posts featuring oversized, unstructured leather totes — particularly in cognac, chocolate, and black. Styled for both office and weekend. Users comparing Bottega Veneta, Loewe, and Celine. High purchase-intent language in comments.
trends.top_post_example: 今年包包不要硬的了，软软的大托特才是正解，装得下MacBook还好看

trends.cluster_summary: Beijing users embracing head-to-toe single-colour winter looks — dominant colours are charcoal, burgundy, and deep forest green. Outerwear is key: long coats, wide-leg trousers, same-tone knitwear. Posts have high saves and outfit recreation requests.
trends.top_post_example: 北京冬天最显高级的穿法：全身同色系，从头到脚酒红色，真的很杀

trends.cluster_summary: Nano and micro bags being styled as accessories rather than functional bags — worn with evening looks or layered over larger totes. Chanel, Dior, and Jacquemus dominate. Posts focus on the bag as a collectible art object, not utility.
trends.top_post_example: 包包不是用来装东西的，是用来装饰自己的 — 今天的迷你包比项链更吸睛

trends.cluster_summary: Beijing users styling heavy, structured blazers as standalone outerwear — replacing traditional coats for transitional weather. Preference for double-breasted styles in wool-cashmere blends. Brands mentioned: Saint Laurent, Givenchy, Max Mara.
trends.top_post_example: 西装外套当大衣穿，北京四月必备，Saint Laurent那件真的封神

trends.cluster_summary: Older, classic leather goods — particularly from the 90s and 2000s — being restyled in contemporary outfits. Hermès Kelly, LV Speedy, Gucci Jackie. Posts are highly aspirational with strong 'investment piece' framing. Significant engagement from 30–45 age group.
trends.top_post_example: 妈妈的Hermès Kelly比我的新包更有味道，经典款就是跨越时代的

trends.cluster_summary: Silk scarves being worn in non-traditional ways — as bag charms, halter tops, hair wraps, and belt replacements. Hermès and Dior dominate, but users also showcasing vintage finds. Tutorial-style posts are driving saves and reposts.
trends.top_post_example: 一条Hermès丝巾的12种戴法，最后一种系在包上绝了

trends.cluster_summary: Beijing's cold climate driving high engagement with fur-trim and shearling details on coats, boots, and bags. Mix of real and faux fur. Brands: Fendi, Miu Miu, Max Mara. Posts lean glamorous and unapologetic — strong contrast to Shanghai's quiet luxury trend.
trends.top_post_example: 北京的冬天就该这么穿，毛领大衣走在街上回头率200%

trends.cluster_summary: A macro-narrative emerging across Shanghai posts: buying fewer, better pieces framed explicitly as financial investments. Content includes 'cost per wear' calculations, capsule wardrobe planning, and brand ROI comparisons. Strong crossover with personal finance community.
trends.top_post_example: 我的穿衣哲学：一件Loro Piana顶十件快时尚，算下来其实更省钱

trends.cluster_summary: Chain-strap crossbody bags surging in Beijing — worn both dressed up and with casual streetwear. Key styles: Chanel Classic Flap, Valentino Loco, Prada Re-Edition. Posts show high styling versatility and strong purchase intent. Younger demographic (22–32) dominant.
trends.top_post_example: 链条包真的百搭，今天配运动裤都好看，Chanel这个颜色今年必入
